objective: Make the public LifeMax app visibly functional and credible through browser-local flows, proof artifacts, Git push, and Vercel deployment while preserving the truth boundary that backend/shared-context readiness remains unproved.
approvalStatus: Oliver explicitly approved production promotion earlier in this goal thread with PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote; this packet excludes credential edits, auth-setting changes, backend activation, DNS/custom-domain mutation, and destructive service changes.
preActionEvidence: The deploy-ready public repo was separate from the dirty LifeMax lab/control-plane repo.
The public app was already deployed, but Oliver's high-functionality reaction required additional visible local behavior rather than static proof.
Fresh LifeMax lab preflight and boot packet remained red, so only browser-local demo behavior could be claimed.

This packet records browser-local demo deployment proof only; credential changes, backend/service activation, auth setting edits, and DNS/custom-domain mutation are not part of this update.

commit_hash_note: This proof packet is tracked, so the final commit hash that contains this packet is recorded in the final Codex response rather than embedded self-referentially here.
residual_risks: Preview and production deployments were performed after explicit approval; no DNS/custom-domain mutation, credential change, auth setting edit, or backend/service activation was performed.
This is still a browser-local demo shell, not the full LifeMax dogfood product.
WHOOP OAuth, account connection, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, database persistence, and backend writes are not connected.
npm install/build output reported 2 moderate dependency audit findings; no forced dependency upgrades were applied.
The lab repo shared-context preflight has previously been red, so no broader daily-usability or wellness-intensity claims are made.
Midday Rescue is browser-local only: it does not send reminders, write to a backend, change integrations, or provide medical advice.
The latest lab boot packet/preflight remain red; broader LifeMax shared-context daily usability is still not complete.
Quick Restart is browser-local only: it does not send reminders, mutate a recovery state, write to a backend, or change integrations.
next_actions: Resolve Vercel preview protection or project split hygiene if preview URLs need to be public proof surfaces.
Keep production promotion/domains under explicit approval even though this run had an approval record.
Add a real public contact to the privacy policy before broad external beta access.
Configure real integrations only through separate approval-gated work.
